title: salix × grayi
intro: Salix × grayi, also known as Gray's willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is a hybrid of the weeping willow (Salix babylonica) and the gray willow (Salix cinerea). The bark of Salix × grayi has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Salix × grayi can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
